steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that furnaces and washing machines use subtypes of austenitic stainless steel. It also states that austenitic stainless steel has a face-centered cubic crystalline structure.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘furnaces and stainless steel used to manufacture washing machines will both have a face-centered cubic crystalline structure.’: Both appliances use austenitic steel, which the passage identifies as having a face-centered cubic crystalline structure. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘washing machines will have a higher concentration of nickel in its composition than stainless steel used to manufacture furnaces will.’: The passage says the 300 series, used for furnaces, has more nickel than the 200 series, used for washing machines, so this reverses the relationship. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘induction cookers will have a face-centered cubic crystalline structure, but stainless steel used to manufacture storage containers will not.’: Induction cookers use ferritic steel, not austenitic steel, and storage containers use the 300 series of austenitic steel. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘storage containers and stainless steel used to manufacture induction cookers will have a similar concentration of nitrogen in their compositions.’: The passage gives no basis for equating nitrogen levels between austenitic and ferritic types. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
